content.problemStatement:
Nature photographing may be fun for tourists, but it is one of the most complicated things for photographers. To capture all the facets of a bird, you might need more than one camera. You recently encountered such a situation.

There are nnn photographers, so there are nnn cameras in a line on the x-axis. All the cameras are at distinct coordinates.

You want to pair up these cameras (nnn is even) in such a way that the sum of angles subtended on the bird by the pair of cameras is maximized. Formally, let AAA, BBB be two cameras, and let PPP be the bird to be captured by these two cameras. The angle will be ∠APB\angle APB∠APB.

**Note:** All angles are in radians.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer nnn.
The second line of each test case contains nnn space-separated integers denoting the xix_ixi​ coordinates of the cameras.
The third line of each test case contains two space-separated integers PPP, QQQ denoting the x and y coordinates of the bird respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output your answer in a single line. Your answer would be considered correct if its absolute error is less than or equal to 1e−61e-61e−6 of the actual answer.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains an integer n. The second line of each test case contains n space-separated integers denoting the x coordinates of the cameras. The third line of each test case contains two space-separated integers denoting the x and y coordinates of the bird.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the maximum sum of angles subtended by the pair of cameras in radians. The absolute error should be less than or equal to 1e-6 of the actual answer.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there are only two cameras, so they must be paired together. The angle subtended by them is 45 degrees, which is approximately 0.785398 radians. For the second test case, the cameras are at x coordinates 0 and 1, and the bird is at (100, 1). The angle subtended by the cameras is very small, approximately 0.000100 radians.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the angle subtended by each pair of cameras and find the maximum sum of these angles. We can use the atan2 function to calculate the angle between two points. We will iterate over all pairs of cameras, calculate the angle subtended by each pair, and keep track of the maximum sum of angles.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) — where T is the number of test cases and n is the number of cameras. This is because we are iterating over all pairs of cameras for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we are using a constant amount of space to store the maximum sum of angles and other variables.

code.Python3:
import math
for _ in range(int(input())):
    n = int(input())
    x = list(map(float, input().split()))
    p, q = map(float, input().split())
    max_sum = 0
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            angle1 = math.atan2(q, x[i] - p)
            angle2 = math.atan2(q, x[j] - p)
            sum = abs(angle1 - angle2)
            if sum > max_sum:
                max_sum = sum
    print(f"{max_sum:.6f}")
